steps.title: Identify two points on the line
steps.content: From the graph, identify two lattice points the line passes through. Two clear ones are $(2,\,4)$ and $(9,\,9)$. Substitute each into each answer choice to see which equation is satisfied by both.

steps.title: Test the equations
steps.content:
Compute the slope: $m = \\dfrac{9-4}{9-2} = \\dfrac{5}{7}$. Then use point-slope form with $(2,4)$: $y - 4 = \\dfrac{5}{7}(x - 2)$, giving $y = \\dfrac{5}{7}x + \\dfrac{18}{7}$, so the slope is $\\dfrac{5}{7}$ and the $y$-intercept is $\\dfrac{18}{7}$.

Verify $(2,4)$ in $35x - 49y = -126$: $35(2) - 49(4) = 70 - 196 = -126$ ✓
Verify $(9,9)$: $35(9) - 49(9) = 315 - 441 = -126$ ✓

Choices A, C, and D either produce a different slope or fail at these points.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
